nonBlockingIssues.description: _compute_atr() duplicated in 4 detectors (mss.py, displacement.py, htf_liquidity.py, liquidity_sweep.py); candidate for extraction to _common.py
disp_key 'atr1.5_br0.6' hardcoded rather than derived from config params
Module docstring mentions state machine (ACTIVE->MITIGATED/INVALIDATED/EXPIRED) not actually implemented; aspirational only
Detection ID uses primitive='ob' while class has primitive_name='order_block' — naming inconsistency

Latent config TF key mismatch: detector uses 'H1'/'H4'/'D1' but config uses '1H'/'4H'/'1D'. Per-TF params silently fall through to defaults. Produces correct results on 5-day dataset but will diverge on different data.
OTE detector has leftover development comments and unused break_price variable
PWH/PWL valid_from hardcoded to '2024-01-07T17:00:00' (dataset-specific, matching pipeline behavior)
test_no_sweeps_before_valid_from is a no-op test (iterates but asserts nothing)
extract_locked_params_for_cascade(config) ignores its config parameter and hardcodes all values
Unused imports in cascade.py: Detection (line 23), get_locked_params (line 549)
configs/locked_baseline.yaml omits 'displacement' from liquidity_sweep upstream; auto-compensated by CascadeEngine.required_upstream() merge

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: TF key naming must be consistent between config YAML (per_tf sections) and detector internal labels. Document that YAML uses '1H'/'4H'/'1D' notation while some detectors use 'H1'/'H4'/'D1'. Recommend standardizing on one form.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: HTF Liquidity detector uses 'H1'/'H4'/'D1' keys (matching pipeline internal labels) but locked_baseline.yaml per_tf dicts use '1H'/'4H'/'1D'. This caused all per-TF config lookups to silently fall through to defaults. Currently not blocking because defaults happen to produce correct results on the 5-day dataset.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Detection ID primitive prefix should match the detector's primitive_name. Document the expected mapping (e.g., order_block -> 'ob' or 'order_block') to prevent inconsistencies.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: OrderBlockDetector has primitive_name='order_block' but generates Detection IDs with primitive='ob'. Other detectors may have similar inconsistencies.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note under 'Shared Detector Utilities': _compute_atr() is duplicated across 4 detectors and is a candidate for extraction to _common.py alongside the already-extracted PIP, SESSION_MAP, map_session, bar_time_str.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mss.py:31, displacement.py:89, htf_liquidity.py:52, liquidity_sweep.py:49 all define identical _compute_atr() functions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: ra-engine-worker skill should mention: (1) composite detector tests need to use the exact same upstream param format as upstream detector tests, not construct from YAML schema; (2) Detection objects store price on Detection.price, not in properties dict; (3) upstream detector output formats should be verified empirically before implementing extractors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: MSS worker noted Detection.price access was a gotcha (mss-detector handoff). OB worker noted param format inconsistency between MSS tests and config schema (order-block-detector handoff). Sweep worker spent time discovering output field names empirically (liquidity-sweep-detector handoff).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: dataset-specific temporal anchors (e.g., PWH/PWL valid_from date) are currently hardcoded to match the 5-day dataset pipeline behavior. These should be documented as known limitations that need dynamic computation for generalization to other date ranges.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: liquidity_sweep.py:448 hardcodes '2024-01-07T17:00:00' matching pipeline line 1617. Build plan specifies 'valid_from: 17:00 NY on Sunday' implying dynamic computation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Fix configs/locked_baseline.yaml: add 'displacement' to liquidity_sweep upstream list in dependency_graph section. The CascadeEngine auto-compensates via required_upstream() merge, but the config should accurately reflect the actual dependency.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: configs/locked_baseline.yaml:59-60 lists [session_liquidity, reference_levels, htf_liquidity, swing_points] but LiquiditySweepDetector.required_upstream() includes 'displacement'. Worker documented this as discoveredIssue in cascade-engine-integration handoff.

rejectedObservations.observation: OTE detector does not have a baseline fixture for field-level regression comparison
rejectedObservations.reason: By design — OTE is a derived composite where the mission spec defines structural validation (zone count, fib ordering, kill zone gate) rather than per-field baseline matching. No OTE baseline fixture was included in the 32 fixture set.

rejectedObservations.observation: extract_locked_params_for_cascade(config) hardcodes values instead of extracting from config object
rejectedObservations.reason: This is test/utility infrastructure code for the locked baseline regression, not a production detector. The AGENTS.md convention about config-driven params applies to detector modules, not test scaffolding. The hardcoded values match the locked_baseline.yaml and are tested by the 52 regression tests.
